steps.title: Identify the sentence structure and the subject
steps.content: The sentence is “In a 2023 article, [___] asserts that...” where the blank provides the subject. “Physicist Mishkat Bhattacharya” forms the complete subject — “physicist” is a descriptor directly attached to the name without an article, so they form a single noun phrase.

steps.title: Apply the rule against subject-verb commas
steps.content: No comma should separate a subject from its verb. (A) and (D) both violate this by placing a comma after the subject before “asserts.” (B) incorrectly breaks the subject into two parts with a comma. (C) correctly connects the complete subject directly to the verb without intervening punctuation. That matches choice C — C is correct.
